mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or twenty-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding two-tenths of a percentage point signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering recession fears and risk-off equity repricing that depresses forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive twenty-day returns in more than sixty percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a three-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in consumer credit balances and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting near-term corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative information coefficient over any rolling twelve-month period, or forward returns do not significantly exceed the unconditional mean during co-expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, rising industrial production reflects firm pricing power and operating leverage, expanding profit margins and boosting S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with sixty-day forward SPX returns during identified supply-constrained regimes, or the signal fails to outperform a simple momentum baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index breaching the three hundred threshold signals entrenched inflationary pressures, prompting market repricing of higher terminal interest rates and compressing forward equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds three hundred, or the signal exhibits no predictive power for negative returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly real GDP growth above the historical trend line implies expanding corporate cash flows and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a six-month investment horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling three-year correlation between quarterly GDP growth and sixty-day forward SPX returns falls below zero, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction, increasing wage-push inflation expectations and depressing equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The employment divergence signal shows no predictive power for negative twenty-day SPX returns, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from the sample mean during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During credit boom phases, rapid expansion in consumer credit balances directly fuels consumption-driven earnings growth, yielding immediate positive equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive five-day SPX returns during identified credit booms, or the signal produces a negative rank IC over any two-year rolling window.
